scope_statement: Reconstruct and audit the mechanism-first primary-source evidence used by the Paper 2 search. This is not a systematic review and does not claim exhaustive capture of publications through 2026.

methods.discovery_log_status: The original discovery log was not retained. The queries below were re-executed on 2026-07-13 to reconstruct coverage and verify source identities.
methods.source_rule: Include original peer-reviewed research at DOI or publisher pages, original PMLR or arXiv papers, official GAO performance audits for operational semantics, and Garrido primary domain sources. Do not use reviews, vendor pages, or generic RL performance headlines as evidence.
methods.evidence_rule: A source is retained only for a mechanism, equation or state requirement, structural result, comparator obligation, bound, experimental-design rule, or operational semantic. Its objective and numerical effects do not transfer to the MFSC unless separately validated.
methods.range_rule: No literature parameter range is imported into the MFSC unless the unit and physical referent match. Otherwise the entry is marked domain-blocked or non-transferable.
search_surfaces: INFORMS publisher pages and DOI records
ScienceDirect publisher pages and DOI records
Taylor & Francis publisher pages and DOI records
United States Government Accountability Office report pages
local Garrido primary-source PDFs and thesis extraction
methods.claim_limit: The inventory establishes traceability and identifies gaps. It does not establish H_PI, H_obs, learned value, or retained-learning value in the MFSC.

included_records.citation: Avriel and Williams (1970), The Value of Information and Stochastic Programming
included_records.mechanism: expected value of perfect information
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: EVPI compares planning with and without advance revelation; no MFSC numerical range transfers
included_records.inclusion_rationale: foundational definition and upper-bound logic
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: does not establish deployable observable value or resource matching

included_records.citation: Brown, Smith, and Sun (2010), Information Relaxations and Duality in Stochastic Dynamic Programs
included_records.mechanism: relax nonanticipativity and penalize privileged information
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: dual penalty converts a perfect-information relaxation into an upper bound
included_records.inclusion_rationale: direct method for tighter H_PI ceilings
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: penalty validity must be proved for the frozen DES state and objective

included_records.citation: Balseiro and Brown (2019), Approximations to Stochastic Dynamic Programs via Information Relaxation Duality
included_records.mechanism: approximate-value-function penalties for tighter information relaxations
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: penalized perfect-information bound tied to a greedy heuristic and approximate value function
included_records.inclusion_rationale: shows why raw perfect-information bounds may be weak
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: its application objectives and asymptotic regimes do not transfer; earlier draft attribution to Ye, Zhou, and Zhu was corrected

included_records.citation: Delage, Arroyo, and Ye (2014), The Value of Stochastic Modeling in Two-Stage Stochastic Programs with Cost Uncertainty
included_records.mechanism: value of stochastic modeling relative to a mean-value solution
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: two-stage mixed-integer objective uncertainty; VSS-like comparison
included_records.inclusion_rationale: prevents assuming stochastic control must beat a robust mean-value calendar
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: two-stage cost-uncertainty results are not an MFSC effect-size bound

included_records.citation: Smallwood and Sondik (1973), The Optimal Control of Partially Observable Markov Processes over a Finite Horizon
included_records.mechanism: belief state as the control state of a finite POMDP
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: finite-horizon value is piecewise-linear and convex in current state probabilities
included_records.inclusion_rationale: primary structural source for belief-state sufficiency
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: does not prove repository observations or an ad hoc feature vector are Markov-sufficient

included_records.citation: Chen (2010), Bounds and Heuristics for Optimal Bayesian Inventory Control with Unobserved Lost Sales
included_records.mechanism: Bayesian inventory control under censored sales
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: sales are censored by inventory and the unknown demand parameter enters the posterior
included_records.inclusion_rationale: direct mechanism for learning value under true lost-sales censoring
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: MFSC logs requested orders, so censoring is currently false unless Garrido validates a different information process

included_records.citation: Huh, Janakiraman, Muckstadt, and Rusmevichientong (2009), An Adaptive Algorithm for Finding the Optimal Base-Stock Policy in Lost Sales Inventory Systems with Censored Demand
included_records.mechanism: adaptive learning of a base-stock policy from censored demand
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: structured base-stock comparator and regret/convergence rather than generic RL
included_records.inclusion_rationale: raises the observable-policy comparator bar
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: objective and censoring assumptions require separate MFSC validation

included_records.citation: Zhang, Chao, and Shi (2020), Closing the Gap: A Learning Algorithm for Lost-Sales Inventory Systems with Lead Times
included_records.mechanism: censored lost-sales learning with positive replenishment lead time
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: pipeline decisions have lasting impact; regret is measured against a full-information base-stock benchmark
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supplies the persistence mechanism and a strong structured comparator
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: lead-time distribution and cost objective do not transfer to MFSC canonical ReT

included_records.citation: Chen, Jiang, Zhang, and Zhou (2024), Learning to Order for Inventory Systems with Lost Sales and Uncertain Supplies
included_records.mechanism: simultaneous demand censoring and uncertain supply learning
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: order decisions learn under lost sales and supply uncertainty
included_records.inclusion_rationale: covers an information-and-supply mechanism absent from ordinary replenishment
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: no evidence that MFSC supply yield is unknown or that demand is censored

included_records.citation: Goldberg, Katz-Rogozhnikov, Lu, Sharma, and Squillante (2016), Asymptotic Optimality of Constant-Order Policies for Lost Sales Inventory Models with Large Lead Times
included_records.mechanism: constant-order policies as strong lost-sales comparators
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: asymptotic optimality in a large-lead-time regime
included_records.inclusion_rationale: prevents treating a constant calendar as a weak baseline
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: asymptotic regime is not an MFSC guarantee

included_records.citation: Nagarajan and Rajagopalan (2008), Inventory Models for Substitutable Products: Optimal Policies and Heuristics
included_records.mechanism: structured inventory control under product substitution
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: compatibility and substitution couple product inventories; optimal/heuristic policies are structured
included_records.inclusion_rationale: shows product labels alone do not require a neural controller
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: MFSC compatibility matrix and product demand shares are unavailable

included_records.citation: Chen and Chao (2020), Dynamic Inventory Control with Stockout Substitution and Demand Learning
included_records.mechanism: demand learning with stockout-based substitution
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: unknown primary demand and substitution probabilities induce structured exploration
included_records.inclusion_rationale: establishes a falsifiable learning mechanism and comparator class
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: substitution suitability and probabilities are domain-blocked

included_records.citation: Ozer and Wei (2004), Inventory Control with Limited Capacity and Advance Demand Information
included_records.mechanism: advance demand information before limited-capacity decisions
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: information lead interacts with a binding capacity constraint
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supports the required leading-signal plus scarce-resource conjunction
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: MFSC signal lead and reservation authority are not documented

included_records.citation: Boada-Collado, Chopra, and Smilowitz (2022), The Value of Information and Flexibility with Temporal Commitments
included_records.mechanism: information and flexible capacity under temporal commitments
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: commitment timing and flexibility determine information value
included_records.inclusion_rationale: direct source for delayed or locked commitment as adaptive headroom mechanism
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: does not validate MFSC activation delay, dwell, cancellation, or capacity units

included_records.citation: Avci, Gokbayrak, and Nadar (2020), Structural Results for Average-Cost Inventory Models with Markov-Modulated Demand and Partial Information
included_records.mechanism: belief-dependent inventory control under a latent demand regime
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: posterior over a Markov regime enters the structured average-cost policy
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supplies a non-neural belief-policy comparator
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: MFSC transition matrix, observation kernel and regime persistence are unavailable

included_records.citation: Paterson, Teunter, and Glazebrook (2012), Enhanced Lateral Transshipments in a Multi-Location Inventory System
included_records.mechanism: dynamic lateral inventory pooling across locations
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: location imbalance and transfer decisions generate transshipment value
included_records.inclusion_rationale: mechanism source for spatial recourse
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: MFSC lateral lane, lift and lead time are absent and direct central dispatch may dominate

included_records.citation: Olsson (2015), Emergency Lateral Transshipments in a Two-Location Inventory System with Positive Transshipment Leadtimes
included_records.mechanism: emergency lateral transshipment with nonzero delay
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: positive transshipment lead time affects the recourse ranking
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supports the explicit transport-delay domination test
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: no transferable MFSC lane or delay range

included_records.citation: Kleywegt, Nori, and Savelsbergh (2004), Dynamic Programming Approximations for a Stochastic Inventory Routing Problem
included_records.mechanism: ADP for joint stochastic inventory-routing decisions
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: inventory state and route decisions are optimized jointly
included_records.inclusion_rationale: requires a strong dynamic routing comparator rather than static routes
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: fleet, payload, turnaround and route graph are MFSC domain gaps

included_records.citation: Abbou and Makis (2019), Group Maintenance: A Restless Bandits Approach
included_records.mechanism: finite repairers allocated among degrading facilities
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: continuous-time Markov degradation, Whittle relaxation and indexability
included_records.inclusion_rationale: establishes Whittle index and relaxation bounds as mandatory comparators
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: near-zero gaps in its numerical systems do not transfer to MFSC

included_records.citation: Sanoubar, de Jonge, Maillart, and Prokopyev (2023), Optimal Condition-Based Maintenance via a Mobile Maintenance Resource
included_records.mechanism: one mobile resource chooses travel, idle, positioning and repair
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: graph location plus Markov asset condition form the MDP state
included_records.inclusion_rationale: matches scarcity, travel commitment and state-dependent ranking reversal
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: MFSC crew count, skills, travel and repair distributions remain unvalidated

included_records.citation: Bai and Yun (1996), Optimal Allocation of Inspection Effort in a Serial Multi-Stage Production System
included_records.mechanism: limited inspection machines allocated by location and inspection fraction
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: production rate is constrained by scarce inspection capacity
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supplies a real throughput-versus-information tradeoff
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: MFSC inspection sensitivity, specificity and inspector-hours are unavailable

included_records.citation: Tang (1991), Designing an Optimal Production System with Inspection
included_records.mechanism: joint inspection and production-system design
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: inspection and capacity choices are coupled
included_records.inclusion_rationale: prevents treating inspection as a free signal
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: cost and defect structure do not transfer

included_records.citation: Bendavid and Herer (2009), Economic Optimization of Off-Line Inspection in a Process that also Produces Non-Conforming Units When in Control and Conforming Units When Out of Control
included_records.mechanism: inspection/disposition under an imperfectly informative process state
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: in-control and out-of-control states can both produce conforming and nonconforming output
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supports explicit false classification and latent quality state
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: MFSC current kernel detects generated defects perfectly and exposes no inspection action

included_records.citation: Akcay and Xu (2004), Joint Inventory Replenishment and Component Allocation Optimization in an Assemble-to-Order System
included_records.mechanism: joint component replenishment and allocation
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: two-stage stochastic integer program; allocation subproblem is multidimensional knapsack
included_records.inclusion_rationale: defines conservation and an exact comparator family
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: MFSC component identities, BOMs and inventories are aggregated away

included_records.citation: Plambeck (2008), Asymptotically Optimal Control for an Assemble-to-Order System with Capacitated Component Production and Fixed Transport Costs
included_records.mechanism: capacitated component production with transport cost and dynamic sequencing
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: component production, expedition/shipping and order sequencing are coupled
included_records.inclusion_rationale: raises comparator bar to structured threshold or Brownian controls
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: MFSC component transport and setup parameters are unavailable

included_records.citation: Reiman, Wan, and Wang (2023), Asymptotically Optimal Inventory Control for Assemble-to-Order Systems
included_records.mechanism: multistage component replenishment and allocation
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: policies depend on BOMs, component balances, backlogs and outstanding lead times
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supplies a strong multistage stochastic-program comparator
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: asymptotic optimality and system parameters do not transfer

included_records.citation: Ata (2006), Dynamic Control of a Multiclass Queue with Thin Arrival Streams
included_records.mechanism: admission and sequencing in a due-date multiclass queue
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: rigid due-date lead times, rejection with lost revenue, and nested threshold policy
included_records.inclusion_rationale: provides operational admission authority and a strong threshold comparator
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: MFSC has backlog but no validated mission expiry or rejection authority

included_records.citation: Long, Shimkin, Zhang, and Zhang (2020), Dynamic Scheduling of Multiclass Many-Server Queues with Abandonment: The Generalized c-mu/h Rule
included_records.mechanism: multiclass priority under abandonment
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: abandonment-sensitive generalized priority rule
included_records.inclusion_rationale: requires a strong classical scheduling comparator
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: MFSC patience distributions and abandonment costs are unavailable

included_records.citation: Zipkin (1984), On the Imbalance of Inventories in Multi-Echelon Systems
included_records.mechanism: inventory imbalance across echelons
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: allocation and echelon state create structural imbalance
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supports explicit echelon inventory and pipeline state
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: information lag alone cannot improve unchanged actions

included_records.citation: Moinzadeh (2002), A Multi-Echelon Inventory System with Information Exchange
included_records.mechanism: information exchange in a multi-echelon inventory system
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: inventory information changes ordering or allocation only through a defined authority
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supports the information-timing and ownership test
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: MFSC separate authorities and reporting-delay distribution are unavailable

included_records.citation: Morris (1991), Factorial Sampling Plans for Preliminary Computational Experiments
included_records.mechanism: elementary-effects screening
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: factorial sampling identifies influential factors in computational experiments
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supports sparse screening before expensive optimization
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: input distributions and ranges must be frozen from the domain

included_records.citation: Sobol (2001), Global Sensitivity Indices for Nonlinear Mathematical Models and Their Monte Carlo Estimates
included_records.mechanism: variance-based global sensitivity
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: S_i=Var(E[H|X_i])/Var(H) and related total effects
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supports interaction and connected-region screening
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: indices are meaningful only under explicit input distributions

included_records.citation: Postek and den Hertog (2016), Multistage Adjustable Robust Mixed-Integer Optimization via Iterative Splitting of the Uncertainty Set
included_records.mechanism: integer and continuous decision rules adapted to revealed uncertainty
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: uncertainty-set partitions preserve nonanticipativity while permitting later actions to differ
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supplies a fair robust adaptive comparator
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: uncertainty sets and decision-rule approximations require a frozen contract

included_records.citation: Lorca, Sun, Litvinov, and Zheng (2016), Multistage Adaptive Robust Optimization for the Unit Commitment Problem
included_records.mechanism: ramping and commitment under sequentially revealed uncertainty
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: intertemporal ramp and unit-commitment constraints make timing causal
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supports persistence, activation delay and robust comparator obligations
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: power-system units and uncertainty ranges do not transfer

included_records.citation: Cui, Long, Qi, and Zhang (2023), The Inventory Routing Problem Under Uncertainty
included_records.mechanism: adaptive inventory routing under distributional ambiguity
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: finite-horizon exact algorithms produce decisions that vary with realized demand
included_records.inclusion_rationale: requires a resource-matched exact or robust routing comparator
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: uses a service-violation objective, not canonical ReT

included_records.citation: Adulyasak, Cordeau, and Jans (2015), Benders Decomposition for Production Routing Under Demand Uncertainty
included_records.mechanism: joint production, setup, inventory and routing under uncertainty
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: two-stage and multistage formulations use Benders and rollout
included_records.inclusion_rationale: requires joint rather than separately tuned comparators
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: MFSC setup, payload and fleet ledgers are absent

included_records.citation: Zhang, Luo, Baldacci, and Lim (2021), A Benders Decomposition Approach for the Multivehicle Production Routing Problem with Order-up-to-Level Policy
included_records.mechanism: multivehicle production routing with order-up-to replenishment
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: exact Benders comparator couples production, visits and vehicle decisions
included_records.inclusion_rationale: sets a strong classical optimization baseline
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: fleet capacity and route data are domain-blocked

included_records.citation: Chitsaz, Cordeau, and Jans (2019), A Unified Decomposition Matheuristic for Assembly, Production, and Inventory Routing
included_records.mechanism: integrated assembly, inventory and routing
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: setup and vehicle subproblems are conserved in one optimization
included_records.inclusion_rationale: prevents weak decomposed baselines
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: its objective and data do not transfer

included_records.citation: GAO (2015), Defense Logistics: Improvements Needed to Accurately Assess the Performance of DOD's Materiel Distribution Pipeline, GAO-15-226
included_records.mechanism: end-to-end military distribution measurement
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: logistics response time, customer wait time, cost and final-destination coverage are separate ledgers
included_records.inclusion_rationale: official primary audit of operational metric requirements
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: provides semantics, not MFSC parameter ranges or an adaptive-policy effect

included_records.citation: GAO (2021), Defense Transportation: DOD Can Better Leverage Existing Contested Mobility Studies and Improve Training, GAO-21-125
included_records.mechanism: mobility under contested operations
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: implementation tracking and training are readiness conditions
included_records.inclusion_rationale: prevents reducing contested logistics to a larger stochastic delay
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: does not validate any MFSC risk frequency or impact

included_records.citation: GAO (2021), Defense Logistics: Army Should Ensure New System Operates in All Situations and Soldiers Complete Training, GAO-21-313
included_records.mechanism: asset visibility and operation under disconnected conditions
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: information availability depends on communications and user/system operation
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supports explicit observer, timing and communications semantics
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: does not establish a specific MFSC signal or observation kernel

included_records.citation: Boesel, Nelson, and Kim (2003), Using Ranking and Selection to Clean Up after Simulation Optimization
included_records.mechanism: selection-bias correction after large simulation search
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: subset selection plus indifference-zone sampling targets prespecified probability of correct or near-best selection
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supports fresh validation after adaptive cell search
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: does not choose the project's 0.01 ReT practical threshold

included_records.citation: Cobbe, Hesse, Hilton, and Schulman (2019), Leveraging Procedural Generation to Benchmark Reinforcement Learning
included_records.mechanism: generalization to unseen procedurally generated environments
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: training and test generator diversity are explicit benchmark variables
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supports disjoint generator universes
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: does not establish any RL advantage in the DES

included_records.citation: Justesen, Torrado, Bontrager, Khalifa, Togelius, and Risi (2018), Illuminating Generalization in Deep Reinforcement Learning through Procedural Level Generation
included_records.mechanism: procedural distribution affects RL generalization
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: generalization depends on the level-generator distribution
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supports freezing and separating tape generators
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: game-level findings are method evidence only

included_records.citation: Powers, Xing, Kolve, Mottaghi, and Gupta (2022), CORA: Benchmarks, Baselines, and Metrics as a Platform for Continual Reinforcement Learning Agents
included_records.mechanism: continual evaluation, forgetting and forward transfer metrics
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: CORA separates continual evaluation, isolated forgetting and zero-shot forward transfer
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supports separate retention and transfer outcomes
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: Paper 3 remains prohibited until learned Paper 2 value exists

included_records.citation: Kaplanis, Shanahan, and Clopath (2019), Policy Consolidation for Continual Reinforcement Learning
included_records.mechanism: regularization-based policy retention across changing tasks
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: multiple policy timescales reduce catastrophic forgetting
included_records.inclusion_rationale: motivates persistent-weight and frozen-policy controls
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: does not prove organizational learning or matched-compute transfer

included_records.citation: Kaplanis, Shanahan, and Clopath (2018), Continual Reinforcement Learning with Complex Synapses
included_records.mechanism: multi-timescale synaptic retention
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: complex synapses trade plasticity against retention
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supports explicit forgetting/plasticity measurement
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: architecture performance is not an MFSC retained-value result

included_records.citation: Caccia, Mueller, Kim, Charlin, and Fakoor (2023), Task-Agnostic Continual Reinforcement Learning: Gaining Insights and Overcoming Challenges
included_records.mechanism: task-agnostic recurrent replay under limited data and compute
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: replay and recurrence affect adaptation and forgetting
included_records.inclusion_rationale: supports matched data, compute and replay controls
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: benchmark superiority does not transfer to recurrent disruption campaigns

included_records.citation: Knoblauch, Husain, and Diethe (2020), Optimal Continual Learning Has Perfect Memory and Is NP-Hard
included_records.mechanism: computational and memory limits of continual learning
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: optimal continual learning generally requires perfect memory and solves an NP-hard problem
included_records.inclusion_rationale: requires replay/memory baselines and limits regularization-only claims
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: the theorem is not a Paper 3 impossibility result for the finite experiment

included_records.citation: Garrido, Ponguta, and Garcia-Reyes (2025; online 2024), Zero-Inventory Plans, Constant Workforce, or Hybrid Approach? Analysing Pure Production Strategies for Enhancing Factory Resilience with Demand Variability
included_records.mechanism: factory aggregate-production-planning strategies evaluated by a Cobb-Douglas resilience construct
included_records.equation_or_parameter_evidence: logit(R)=0.024 ln(zeta)-0.026 ln(epsilon)+0.040 ln(phi)-0.060 ln(tau)-0.1771 ln(kappa_dot); demand variability is the uncertainty; the reported preferred strategy is fixed
included_records.inclusion_rationale: authoritative source for the exact factory construct requested by Garrido
included_records.exclusion_or_limit: not the canonical order-level MFSC endpoint, not spatially calibrated, and not evidence of adaptive-over-open-loop headroom

excluded_or_not_promoted.rationale: Useful for discovery only; every retained scientific claim is tied to original research or an official primary audit.
No headline comparison is portable across objective, information, resources, action rights, comparator strength, or tape generator.
Lead times, crew counts, repair factors, patience, signal accuracy, fleet capacity and costs are not imported without matching MFSC units and operational referents.
The thesis product is approximately three-year nonperishable; short shelf-life mechanisms contradict the current product contract.
The Garrido index is retained as a factory construct sensitivity, not as a post hoc replacement for ret_excel_visible_v1.
Continual-learning papers inform controls only; Paper 3 is unauthorized until Paper 2 establishes learned adaptive value.
The reconstructive search surfaced newer continual-RL and simulation-optimization papers, but they do not close a recorded mechanism gap or change a current gate. A systematic update remains open.

coverage_gaps.gap: The original discovery dates, databases, result counts and screening decisions were not retained.
coverage_gaps.impact: The review cannot claim systematic or exhaustive coverage.
coverage_gaps.closure_evidence: A preregistered database search with exported results, deduplication, title/abstract screening and full-text exclusion reasons.

coverage_gaps.gap: Some paywalled sources were verified from publisher metadata/abstracts rather than a newly archived full-text copy.
coverage_gaps.impact: Only the structural evidence stated in the inventory is retained; no unverified coefficient or range is imported.
coverage_gaps.closure_evidence: Archive and hash legally accessible full texts, then record page/equation locators.

coverage_gaps.gap: No verified MFSC-compatible imperfect-repair factor or post-repair state distribution was found.
coverage_gaps.impact: Imperfect repair remains domain-blocked and cannot be introduced by tuning.
coverage_gaps.closure_evidence: Garrido or a directly applicable military maintenance source supplies the physical state, units and plausible range.

coverage_gaps.gap: Fleet count, payload, turnaround, lateral lanes, convoy contention and signal ownership are unavailable.
coverage_gaps.impact: Inventory-routing and lateral-transshipment classes are embeddability hypotheses, not licensed MFSC contracts.
coverage_gaps.closure_evidence: Validated Garrido facts with a conservation ledger and pre-action observation timing.

coverage_gaps.gap: Twenty-one product demand shares, BOMs, compatibility/substitution, component inventory and mixed-load lift are unavailable.
coverage_gaps.impact: Product-mix and component-kit mechanisms remain blocked.
coverage_gaps.closure_evidence: Named products/components, BOMs, suitability matrix, inventories, lead times and authority.

coverage_gaps.gap: Latent quality states, inspection sensitivity/specificity, inspector-hours and unqualified-output consequences are unavailable.
coverage_gaps.impact: Inspection has zero action liveness in the current kernel and the extension is unvalidated.
coverage_gaps.closure_evidence: A Garrido-approved quality and inspection ledger with a null regime.

coverage_gaps.gap: Mission expiry, patience distributions, class values and rejection authority are unavailable.
coverage_gaps.impact: Admission/abandonment cannot be embedded without inventing demand shedding.
coverage_gaps.closure_evidence: Operational deadlines and a lost-demand ledger approved before results.

coverage_gaps.gap: No validated latent-regime transition probabilities, leading observation kernel, activation delay or minimum dwell were found.
coverage_gaps.impact: A regime-switching reservation contract remains a disclosed researcher extension requiring sign-off.
coverage_gaps.closure_evidence: Primary operational data or Garrido-approved ranges and a falsification cell.

coverage_gaps.gap: The GAO sources are official performance audits, not a comprehensive current military-logistics doctrine review.
coverage_gaps.impact: They support observability and readiness semantics only.
coverage_gaps.closure_evidence: A separately scoped search of current official doctrine and technical manuals tied to the modeled echelon.

coverage_gaps.gap: The retained papers do not directly validate persistent-versus-reset learning under identical recurrent-campaign multisets, information and total compute in this DES.
coverage_gaps.impact: They set control obligations but cannot authorize Paper 3.
coverage_gaps.closure_evidence: First confirm learned Paper 2 value, then preregister the exact persistent/reset/frozen/scratch estimand and virgin campaign design.

coverage_gaps.gap: The reconstructive search was targeted and did not systematically screen all 2025-2026 publications across bibliographic databases.
coverage_gaps.impact: No claim of current exhaustive literature coverage is permitted.
coverage_gaps.closure_evidence: A dated systematic update with database exports and inclusion flow.

audit_corrections.correction: DOI 10.1287/opre.2018.1782 was attributed to Ye, Zhou, and Zhu in the review; publisher metadata identifies Santiago R. Balseiro and David B. Brown.
audit_corrections.action: Corrected the mechanism matrix and prose attribution.

audit_corrections.correction: Several citations used online-first versus issue-year labels inconsistently.
audit_corrections.action: Standardized Chen to 2010, Boada-Collado et al. to 2022, and Avci et al. to 2020 while retaining Garrido as 2025 with online-2024 disclosure.

audit_corrections.correction: The review invoked POMDP belief sufficiency without a direct foundational source.
audit_corrections.action: Added Smallwood and Sondik (1973) and limited the inference to specified finite-state transition and observation kernels.

audit_corrections.correction: The factory Cobb-Douglas construct was absent from the literature matrix despite being a current metric-governance question.
audit_corrections.action: Added Garrido, Ponguta, and Garcia-Reyes with exact published coefficients and an explicit non-transfer boundary.

validation.link_method: Concurrent GET with redirects from every recorded primary_source_url, followed by publisher/search inspection for access-blocked pages.
validation.interpretation: A 403 is recorded as publisher bot/access blocking only when the DOI or canonical report URL resolved to the expected publisher page. It is not treated as missing source evidence.
validation.metadata_crosscheck: DOI metadata and primary publisher pages were checked for title, authorship and publication year; the audit corrections above were applied to the review.
